label: Legitimate Control Enforcement Overlay
description: TSMM-native overlay for revocation latency, attributable audit, redress proportionality, and designated accountability.

nodes.label: Legitimate control overlay domain
nodes.description: Domain where control-side mechanisms must operate at execution speed.

nodes.label: Accountable operator
nodes.description: Named operator with authority to invoke revocation, audit, and redress primitives.

nodes.label: Legitimate control policy
nodes.description: Specifies control-side thresholds for deployed systems.

nodes.label: Controlled service
nodes.description: Service whose actions must remain interruptible, attributable, and remediable.

nodes.label: Control readiness assessment
nodes.description: Assesses whether the service satisfies the overlay thresholds.

nodes.label: Control gate decision
nodes.description: Allows, restricts, or escalates service operation based on overlay compliance.

nodes.label: Legitimate control receipt
nodes.description: Evidence showing revocation, audit, attribution, and redress readiness.

nodes.label: Control enforcement effect
nodes.description: Applies an operational boundary when the overlay is not met.

nodes.label: Governance designation record
nodes.description: Publishes accountable operator designation and control commitments.

edges.description: Accountable operator defines the legitimate control overlay.
Overlay constrains the service with control-side requirements.
Assessment evaluates the service against the overlay thresholds.
Assessment outcome informs the operational gate.
Gate decision emits a receipt showing whether control requirements were met.
Accountable operator publishes a signed governance designation record.
